reviews.strongestCounterargument: The synchronized 16-request generator and synthetic sublinear service curve are too clean to represent production continuous batching, so the sign reversal may disappear under heterogeneous prompt and decode lengths.
reviews.unsupportedClaimRisk: The weakest claim was that the discovered interaction was confirmatory; it was narrowed to exploratory mechanism evidence because the interaction claim was written only after the original directional hypothesis failed.
reviews.reproductionBarrier: The Node simulation is easy to reproduce, but the consequential external test requires timestamped production traces and a hardware-derived batch service surface that many teams do not yet retain.
revisions: Reclassified the saved run as exploratory mechanism evidence and required a preregistered follow-up on unseen traces and empirical service curves.
Added numeric no-efficiency-control outcomes showing identical 156.3 rps capacity and 28.9% drops rather than merely asserting that the control failed.
Corrected the artifact accounting from 5,040 aggregate cells to 84 aggregates and 5,040 seed-policy rows.

reviews.strongestCounterargument: K3's launch-day coding and agent results may justify faster adoption than this cautious sequence, especially for organizations that only consume the managed API and never intend to host weights.
reviews.unsupportedClaimRisk: The weakest wording treated Moonshot's 64-accelerator recommendation as a hard deployment floor; it was narrowed to provider guidance that does not establish a minimum or cost optimum.
reviews.reproductionBarrier: Harness parity is difficult because published competitors use Kimi Code, Claude Code, Codex, Terminus 2, and mini-SWE-agent, while product tools and preserved thinking history may not be portable.
revisions: Changed the self-host claim from a 64-accelerator floor to a recommended topology and explicitly marked smaller supported configurations as unknown.
Added a randomized incumbent control to the production canary so repository difficulty and traffic mix cannot masquerade as a K3 effect.
Changed open-model release wording to open-model commitment because complete self-hosting artifacts and technical details are not yet equally available.

reviews.strongestCounterargument: A backend matrix may slow adoption of a well-tested framework release, while the article cannot independently verify the MPS and memory launch measurements without running the relevant hardware benchmarks.
reviews.unsupportedClaimRisk: The weakest implication was that the launch speedups justified an early upgrade; it was narrowed to a cell-specific canary and all numbers remain explicitly attributed to PyTorch until target reproduction.
reviews.reproductionBarrier: Organizations need multiple accelerator backends, compiler images, representative checkpoints, and pinned 2.12.1 baselines; a single developer workstation cannot validate distributed and cross-vendor cells.
revisions: Replaced broad early-upgrade guidance with a cell-specific canary boundary and explicit target-SoC reproduction requirement.
Added checkpoint forward- and rollback-reader compatibility as a pre-performance gate, with serialization drift now blocking a row.
Elevated the tracked GPU-less ROCm-wheel compile regression into the reason utility hosts belong in the validation matrix.
